ratio_source_quote: 2:1 (756.6 mg of IND and 243.4 mg of NAP)
process_details: Physical mixture (mortar/pestle 60 s) melted in aluminum dish at 441.15 K for 5 min, then quench cooled by pouring liquid nitrogen onto sample; stored over P2O5 until room temperature; gently milled with mortar and pestle.
source_quote: In the diffractograms of the 2:1 (NAP:IND) mixture distinctive peaks belonging to crystalline NAP appear (Figures 3 and 6) for both storage temperatures. Stability study: stored in desiccators under dry conditions (phosphorus pentoxide) at 277.15 and 298.15 K for 21 days and repeatedly analyzed by XRPD (at days 0, 7, 14 and 21).
notes: Excess NAP recrystallized at 4 °C (Figure 6 shows distinct NAP peak at 19.1° already at 4 °C). Exact day of first appearance not specified - assigned to first interval (day 7) but uncertain.
review_reasons: Exact crystallization onset day not reported (only days 0/7/14/21 measured); induction_time_days is approximate.

ratio_source_quote: 2:1 (756.6 mg of IND and 243.4 mg of NAP)
process_details: Physical mixture (mortar/pestle 60 s) melted in aluminum dish at 441.15 K for 5 min, then quench cooled by pouring liquid nitrogen onto sample; stored over P2O5 until room temperature; gently milled with mortar and pestle.
source_quote: In the diffractograms of the 2:1 (NAP:IND) mixture distinctive peaks belonging to crystalline NAP appear (Figures 3 and 6) for both storage temperatures. The peaks observed for the 2:1 and 1:2 sample are all related to recrystallization of the excess amount of naproxen and indomethacin, respectively.
notes: Excess NAP recrystallized at 25 °C (Figure 6: peaks at 6.8°, 12.8°, 19.0°, 22.5°, 27.4°). Exact onset day not specified.
review_reasons: Exact crystallization onset day not reported (only days 0/7/14/21 measured); induction_time_days is approximate.

ratio_source_quote: 1:1 (608.1 mg of IND and 391.6 mg of NAP)
process_details: Physical mixture (mortar/pestle 60 s) melted in aluminum dish at 441.15 K for 5 min, then quench cooled by pouring liquid nitrogen onto sample; stored over P2O5 until room temperature; gently milled with mortar and pestle.
detection_methods: XRPD (days 0, 7, 14, 21); DSC for Tg; ATR-FTIR (heterodimer evidence)
source_quote: Interestingly the 1:1 mixture stayed amorphous over the period of storage, indicated by the appearance of a halo in the XRPD diffractograms, even though it does not have the highest Tg.
notes: 1:1 sample remained amorphous through 21-day study at 4 °C; no crystallization observed.

ratio_source_quote: 1:1 (608.1 mg of IND and 391.6 mg of NAP)
process_details: Physical mixture (mortar/pestle 60 s) melted in aluminum dish at 441.15 K for 5 min, then quench cooled by pouring liquid nitrogen onto sample; stored over P2O5 until room temperature; gently milled with mortar and pestle.
detection_methods: XRPD (days 0, 7, 14, 21); DSC for Tg; ATR-FTIR (heterodimer evidence)
source_quote: Interestingly the 1:1 mixture stayed amorphous over the period of storage, indicated by the appearance of a halo in the XRPD diffractograms, even though it does not have the highest Tg.
notes: 1:1 sample remained amorphous through 21-day study at 25 °C; FTIR indicates heterodimer formation between NAP and IND carboxylic acid groups.

ratio_source_quote: 1:2 (562.8 mg of IND and 437.2 mg of NAP)
process_details: Physical mixture (mortar/pestle 60 s) melted in aluminum dish at 441.15 K for 5 min, then quench cooled by pouring liquid nitrogen onto sample; stored over P2O5 until room temperature; gently milled with mortar and pestle.
source_quote: the 1:2 (NAP:IND) mixture shows recrystallization of γ-IND only in the samples stored at 298.15 K (Figures 3 and 5).
notes: At 4 °C (277.15 K) the 1:2 sample remained amorphous (only halo visible in Figure 6, top right).

ratio_source_quote: 1:2 (562.8 mg of IND and 437.2 mg of NAP)
process_details: Physical mixture (mortar/pestle 60 s) melted in aluminum dish at 441.15 K for 5 min, then quench cooled by pouring liquid nitrogen onto sample; stored over P2O5 until room temperature; gently milled with mortar and pestle.
source_quote: the 1:2 (NAP:IND) mixture shows recrystallization of γ-IND only in the samples stored at 298.15 K (Figures 3 and 5). The peaks observed for the 2:1 and 1:2 sample are all related to recrystallization of the excess amount of naproxen and indomethacin, respectively.
notes: Excess γ-IND recrystallized at 25 °C (Figure 6: peaks at 11.8°, 17.2°, 22.0°, 26.7°). Exact onset day not specified.
review_reasons: Exact crystallization onset day not reported (only days 0/7/14/21 measured); induction_time_days is approximate.
